title: Fix Kuzu graph path fallback warning
description: GraphStore currently calls the generic directory helper on the Kuzu database path itself. When an existing Kuzu graph path is a file-like database, os.makedirs raises FileExistsError and db.client falls back to graph_store repeatedly. Ensure the parent storage directory instead and verify backend tests.
close_reason: Fixed GraphStore to ensure the parent storage directory instead of the Kuzu graph database path, preserving existing graph paths and avoiding graph_store fallback warnings. Added regression test and verified full backend suite.

title: Migration shim cleanup gate
description: Phase 5 cannot close until `rg 'TODO\(migration\)' backend src-tauri` returns empty. Audit and remove every shim added during Phase 1 or later. This is the gate before Phase 6 starts.

title: Add service-layer tests with fake adapters
description: Create `backend/tests/service/` with tests that exercise each service function using fake/in-memory adapters. No FastAPI test client. No real storage. These become the bulk of regression coverage going forward.

title: Convert FastAPI routes to thin wrappers
description: Each FastAPI route in `backend/main.py` becomes a thin adapter that translates HTTP request -> service call -> HTTP response. Frontend behavior is preserved end-to-end. `main.py` ends up containing route declarations and startup wiring only.

title: Extract service modules: leads, profile, settings, ingestion, scanning, generation
description: Move route bodies from `backend/main.py` into `backend/app/services/{leads,profile,settings,ingestion,scanning,generation}.py`. Services depend on adapters/config/task manager — never on FastAPI `Request`, `UploadFile`, `BackgroundTasks`, or `WebSocket`. Each service function takes plain Python args and returns plain Python data.
notes: Partial service extraction committed: leads/profile/settings/template/events/profile-import/cleanup/pipeline-PDF helpers moved into app.services with full backend tests passing. Remaining route bodies still in main.py are scan/reevaluate, generate/contact lookup, fire/apply/form read, LinkedIn/GitHub/portfolio uploads, and websocket-only broadcasting. Next slice should define an event/callback boundary so services can emit plain events without depending on FastAPI, BackgroundTasks, or WebSocket.

title: Scaffold app/services and app/tasks
description: Create `backend/app/services/` directory and `backend/app/tasks.py`. Tasks module provides a worker pool with cancellation tokens and a task registry interface. Services start as empty modules ready to receive extracted route bodies.
close_reason: Scaffolded backend/app/services modules and backend/app/tasks.py with cancellable task registry; added unit tests; full backend test suite passes.

title: Add import-isolation tests + establish migration shim convention
description: Add tests under `backend/tests/unit/` that import every backend module and assert no real storage is opened. Document the convention that any temporary compatibility shim must include `# TODO(migration): remove in phase 5` so a single grep can verify cleanup before Phase 5 closes.
close_reason: Expanded import-isolation coverage to discover backend library modules and documented the Phase 5 migration shim marker convention in backend README; verified targeted backend suite passes.

title: Build versioned migration runner
description: Add `backend/storage/migrations/` with versioned files and a single migration runner that applies them at startup or first connection. Move all schema creation out of global imports into this runner. Document migration ordering and how to add a new migration.
close_reason: Added versioned SQLite migration package and runner, moved schema creation out of db.client into migration 0001, documented migration ordering, and verified targeted backend suite passes.

title: Implement storage adapters with zero-side-effect imports
description: Build `backend/storage/{sqlite,graph,vectors,settings}.py` as adapter classes/factories. Adapters connect lazily in their `__init__` (or first call), document concurrency model (lock vs connection-per-thread vs single-thread executor), and expose narrow typed APIs. The Settings adapter exposes typed accessors, not string-key `get_setting` calls. No raw SQL or Cypher leaks out of these modules.
close_reason: Added zero-side-effect storage adapter modules for SQLite, graph, vectors, and settings; routed db.client through lazy adapter factories; tagged legacy raw-connection compatibility shims for Phase 5 removal; verified targeted backend suite passes.

title: Audit and remove import-time DB connections
description: Audit `backend/db/client.py` and storage-touching modules for any DB connection or directory creation that runs at import time. Convert to explicit factory functions or lazy adapter constructors. Verify by importing each module in a test that asserts no app data dirs are created and no Kuzu/LanceDB/SQLite connections are opened.
close_reason: Removed import-time Kuzu/LanceDB/SQLite/assets side effects from db.client, generator, and ingestor; added import-isolation regression test; verified targeted backend suite passes under uv.

title: Phase 2: Extract services without changing transport
description: Move route bodies out of backend/main.py into backend/app/services/* (leads, profile, settings, ingestion, scanning, generation). FastAPI routes become thin wrappers around services. Services depend on explicit adapters/config/task manager — never on FastAPI Request/UploadFile/BackgroundTasks/WebSocket. Add app/tasks.py with worker pool and cancellation-token primitives. Exit criteria: main.py mostly contains route declarations and startup wiring, services are callable without FastAPI objects, service tests use fake adapters and do not touch real storage.

title: Phase 1: Remove import-time storage side effects
description: Stop opening Kuzu/LanceDB/SQLite at module import time. Introduce explicit storage adapter objects with zero-side-effect imports. Move schema creation and migrations out of global scope into a versioned migration runner. Establish the convention that any temporary compatibility shim must be tagged '# TODO(migration): remove in phase 5' so a single grep can verify cleanup before Phase 5 closes. Exit criteria: importing backend modules does not open Kuzu/LanceDB/SQLite, tests can construct fake adapters cleanly, all compatibility shims are grep-trackable.
close_reason: Phase 1 complete: backend imports no longer open SQLite/Kuzu/LanceDB, lazy storage adapters and versioned migrations are in place, migration shim marker is documented, and full backend test suite passes.

title: Backend refactor: Tauri-owned stdio JSON-RPC sidecar
description: Replace the local FastAPI/WebSocket sidecar with a Tauri-owned stdio JSON-RPC backend. First remove import-time storage side effects, then extract application logic into services, then introduce JSON-RPC alongside FastAPI, then move React traffic through Rust-bridged Tauri commands, then delete FastAPI. Phases 1-6 are independently revertable; Phase 7 (FastAPI deletion) is the only irreversible step and only happens after a one-week dogfood soak with no backend_crashed events and no feature falling back to REST. Architecture decisions baked in: line-delimited JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdin/stdout, Rust owns request IDs and notification fan-out, initialize handshake negotiates protocol_version + capabilities, long-running ops return task_id and emit correlated notifications, cancellation is best-effort (LSP convention), file transfer via Tauri file picker + path passing (not multipart), pydantic models are the source of truth for IPC schemas with TS types generated via json-schema-to-typescript, MCP server keeps its tool-shaped public schema but shares underlying services with app IPC. Hard rule: domain/ never imports storage/llm/ipc/Tauri/FastAPI/filesystem.

title: Draft lightweight lead harvester PRD
description: Create a PRD/spec capturing the simplified product direction discussed: CLI-first scraping and lead triage, small dashboard, time-aware scans, and Rezi MCP handoff for resume generation.
close_reason: Created spec/spec-design-lightweight-lead-harvester-prd.md with product scope, non-goals, CLI/dashboard workflows, time-aware requirements, Rezi MCP handoff, data contracts, acceptance criteria, and test strategy.

title: Add tests proving MCP and JSON-RPC paths agree
description: Add tests that drive both MCP tool calls and the equivalent JSON-RPC method calls with identical inputs and assert the underlying service produces identical outputs. Catches drift between the two protocols and confirms the shared-service rule is enforced.

title: Refactor MCP handlers to call shared services
description: Refactor `backend/mcp_server.py` handlers (`score_job_fit`, `evaluate_lead_quality`, `extract_lead_intel`) to call shared service functions in `backend/app/services/`. The MCP public schema stays tool-shaped; only the underlying call routes through the same service layer the JSON-RPC backend uses.

title: Round-trip validation of schemas through codegen
description: Add a test that verifies every pydantic IPC model produces a usable JSON Schema and a non-trivial TS type. Reject clever pydantic validators (custom serializers, computed fields) that don't round-trip cleanly. Update the schema-style guide in the IPC module's docstring.

title: Wire codegen into CI with drift detection
description: Add a CI check that regenerates TS types and fails if they differ from the committed version. Choose: either commit generated types to git, or generate at build time. Either is acceptable; just make the choice explicit and stop hand-maintaining TS types.

title: Build pydantic -> JSON Schema -> TS pipeline
description: Add a Python script (or Make target) that walks `backend/ipc/schemas.py` and `backend/ipc/notifications.py`, calls `model.model_json_schema()` on each, writes JSON Schema files, then runs `json-schema-to-typescript` to emit TS types into `src/lib/ipc-types.ts` (or similar). Document where generated types live and how to regenerate.

title: Rust bridge tests including crash path
description: Add Rust tests covering: request/response correlation (multiple in-flight requests), protocol-handshake failure, notification fan-out to React, `$/cancel` propagation, and crash path using a mock Python sidecar that exits on a magic method (assert pending requests reject with 5002).

title: Implement crash recovery (5002, offline state, single respawn)
description: On unexpected child exit: reject all pending requests with stable error code `5002 backend_crashed`, emit a backend-offline state to React, stop forwarding notifications, auto-respawn ONCE with backoff. If respawn fails or replacement crashes immediately, stay offline until user/app restart or explicit retry command.

title: Add Tauri commands and notification fan-out
description: Expose Tauri commands: `backend_invoke(method, params)`, `backend_cancel(task_id|request_id)`, `backend_status()`. Rust reads stdout continuously, matches responses to pending requests by ID, and forwards notifications to React as Tauri events. React subscribes via `listen()`.

title: Implement BackendProcess struct: spawn, pipes, request map, handshake
description: In `src-tauri/src/`, add a `BackendProcess` struct that owns the Python child process, stdin writer, pending-request map keyed by request ID, captured `protocol_version` from initialize handshake, and a notification emitter. Spawning the backend sends `initialize` first with a 2s timeout; failure or timeout is treated as a crash.

title: Wire scan start/stop/progress over JSON-RPC
description: Replace the WebSocket-based scan progress path with JSON-RPC: `scan_start` returns `{task_id}` immediately, then emits `task.progress` notifications, then `task.completed` or `task.failed`. `scan_stop` triggers `$/cancel`. Add notification schemas to contract tests and TS codegen.

title: Implement best-effort $/cancel + stale-result handling
description: Implement `$/cancel` notification handler. Cancellation is best-effort per LSP convention: server may still emit progress and may produce a final result after cancel. Document that clients must handle stale responses via request IDs / task IDs / generation counters. Add tests that intentionally produce stale results and assert clients can ignore them.

title: Implement notification emission with task_id correlation
description: Add `ipc/notifications.py`. Emit `task.started`, `task.progress`, `task.completed`, `task.failed` plus entity-specific notifications like `lead.updated`. Every task notification carries `task_id` so React can correlate. Define notification schemas in `ipc/notifications.py` as pydantic models so they flow through TS codegen.

title: Implement task registry + tasks.list/tasks.get
description: Add a task registry tracking state (running, completed, failed, cancelled), cancellation tokens, timestamps, and task kind. Long-running RPC methods register a task and return `{task_id}` immediately. Add `tasks.list` and `tasks.get` IPC methods so the frontend can query active/recent tasks.

title: Build CLI/stdin contract test harness
description: Add a CLI harness that pipes JSON-RPC lines to the backend and asserts on stdout responses. Cover request schemas, response schemas, and error responses. This becomes the source of truth for IPC contracts.

title: Wire core methods over JSON-RPC
description: Add JSON-RPC method handlers that call the same services FastAPI routes call: health, settings get/save, leads list/detail/update, profile read/write. FastAPI keeps running side-by-side. Each handler reuses the service function, never duplicates logic.

title: Implement initialize handshake with protocol_version + capabilities
description: Add `initialize` method that returns `{protocol_version, capabilities}`. Specify a stable schema for `initialize` itself (it can't be version-gated since it negotiates the version). Define one real capability that may legitimately be off (e.g., `experimental_browser_automation`) — don't ship a capability map that does nothing. Add tests for protocol-version mismatch handling.

title: Implement JSON-RPC stdio loop, dispatch, and error mapping
description: Build `backend/ipc/server.py` (line-delimited JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdin/stdout), `ipc/router.py` (method dispatch table), `ipc/errors.py` (map exceptions to JSON-RPC error codes per the §8 code ranges in the plan). Stderr is the log channel; stdout is JSON-RPC only.

title: MCP server: share services with app IPC instead of duplicating logic
description: backend/mcp_server.py and the new ipc/server.py should not duplicate business logic. Rule: MCP and app IPC may keep different public schemas (MCP is tool-shaped, app IPC is product-shaped) but both must call the same shared services in backend/app/services/* underneath. Refactor mcp_server.py's score_job_fit, evaluate_lead_quality, and extract_lead_intel handlers to call the same service functions the JSON-RPC handlers call. The shared service layer is the source of truth; both protocol adapters translate into it. Do NOT force MCP tools and IPC methods into one external dispatch table — their schemas legitimately differ.

title: Pydantic → JSON Schema → TypeScript codegen pipeline
description: Build the type-generation pipeline that makes pydantic IPC models the single source of truth for both Python validation and TypeScript types on the frontend. Path: pydantic models → JSON Schema (via model.model_json_schema()) → json-schema-to-typescript. Wire it into CI so generated TS lives in version control or is regenerated automatically. Contract tests validate the pydantic schemas; the same schemas drive TS generation. Avoid clever pydantic validators that don't round-trip through JSON Schema. This unblocks the rest of Phase 3 (and especially Phase 4's notification schemas) by giving the frontend stable, free types instead of hand-maintained TS.

title: Phase 5: Add Rust backend bridge
description: Replace port/token state with a BackendProcess struct that owns the Python child process, stdin writer, pending-request map, protocol_version from initialize handshake, and notification emitter. Expose Tauri commands: backend_invoke(method, params), backend_cancel(task_id|request_id), backend_status(). Rust reads stdout continuously and forwards backend notifications to React as Tauri events. Implement crash recovery: on unexpected exit, reject all pending requests with stable error code 5002 backend_crashed, emit backend-offline state to React, auto-respawn ONCE with backoff; if respawn fails or replacement crashes immediately, stay offline until user/app restart. Delete every '# TODO(migration): remove in phase 5' compatibility shim before considering this phase complete. Exit criteria: React can call JSON-RPC through Tauri, backend notifications reach React without WebSocket, no localhost port required for migrated calls, rg 'TODO\\(migration\\)' backend returns empty, backend crash does not hang pending frontend calls.

title: Phase 4: Add task model and notification path
description: Add task registry tracking state, cancellation tokens, timestamps, and task kind. Finalize task and notification schemas. Emit task.started, task.progress, task.completed, task.failed plus entity notifications like lead.updated. Every task notification carries task_id so React can correlate. Implement best-effort $/cancel handling per LSP convention — server may still emit progress or produce a final result after cancel; clients handle stale responses via request IDs / task IDs / generation counters. Add tasks.list and tasks.get methods. Exit criteria: scan start/stop/progress works over JSON-RPC in backend tests, stale cancellation/result behavior is explicitly tested, notification schemas are in contract tests + TS codegen.

title: Phase 3: Add JSON-RPC backend internally (alongside FastAPI)
description: Add backend/ipc/server.py with a line-delimited JSON-RPC 2.0 stdio loop. Implement initialize handshake that returns protocol_version and capabilities. Wire core methods to the same services FastAPI calls: health, settings get/save, leads list/detail/update, profile read/write. FastAPI keeps running side-by-side. Add a CLI/stdin harness for contract tests. Define request/response/error schemas now; defer task and notification schemas to Phase 4. Exit criteria: core methods work over stdio, errors are stable and typed, protocol-version incompatibility is handled cleanly.

title: Remove frontend startup polling + REST fallback
description: Remove the frontend's polling for sidecar port/token at startup (now provided by Rust state, not by sniffing stdout). Remove REST fallback paths from `apiClient`. Verify the app cold-starts entirely through Tauri -> Rust bridge -> Python JSON-RPC with no HTTP server involvement.

title: Remove FastAPI/uvicorn/CORS/token/port/WebSocket from backend
description: Delete `_free_port` retry loop, bearer token generation, CORS middleware, WebSocket support, uvicorn dep, FastAPI dep. Update `backend/pyproject.toml`. Remove related imports across `backend/main.py` (which by now is the slim entry point).

title: Verify dogfood soak gate
description: Phase 7 starts only after seven consecutive days of normal usage on the JSON-RPC path with: zero `backend_crashed` events in observability logs, zero features falling back to REST in observability logs, no blocking regressions reported. Document the soak window and the gate result before opening any deletion PR.

title: Replace useWS with Tauri event subscription hook
description: Delete `src/hooks/useWS.ts` and any port/token/WebSocket logic from the frontend. Replace with a `useBackendEvents` hook that subscribes via `tauri.event.listen`. Verify no frontend code references port, API token, HTTP URL, or WebSocket.

title: Migrate remaining cohorts: profile, ingestion, generation, background events
description: Migrate the remaining feature groups to JSON-RPC: profile, ingestion, generation/application, and any remaining background event subscribers. Each migration follows the same pattern: switch the apiClient flag, verify transport=rpc in logs, smoke test.

title: Migrate scan start/stop/progress (streaming validation)
description: Migrate the scanning feature to JSON-RPC early in Phase 6 to prove task_id correlation and notification fan-out end-to-end. UI subscribes to backend Tauri events instead of WebSocket. This is the streaming smoke test for the whole architecture.

title: Migrate first cohort: health, settings, leads
description: Migrate health/startup, settings read/write, and leads list/detail/update from REST to JSON-RPC. Verify via observability logs that all calls show `transport=rpc` post-migration. No regressions in existing UI behavior.

title: Build apiClient abstraction with dual-mode transport
description: Replace centralized `ApiFetch` wrapper in `src/App.tsx` with an `apiClient` that can call either REST (legacy) or JSON-RPC (via `tauri.invoke('backend_invoke', ...)`) per feature. Per-feature transport is a flag the migration toggles. Every call logs `transport=rest|rpc`, method/feature, duration, success/failure, and error code if present.

title: Phase 7: Delete FastAPI transport (only after dogfood soak)
description: Phase 7 starts ONLY after a one-week dogfood soak on the JSON-RPC path with no backend_crashed events in the migration logs and no feature falling back to REST. This is the only irreversible phase. Remove _free_port retry loop, bearer token generation, CORS middleware, WebSocket support, uvicorn, FastAPI dependencies. Remove frontend startup polling for sidecar port/token. Remove REST fallback paths from apiClient. Keep service, storage, IPC, bridge, and contract tests. Exit criteria: app runs entirely over Tauri → Rust bridge → Python JSON-RPC, FastAPI/WebSocket/port/token code is gone, no dual-transport fallback remains.

title: Phase 6: Migrate frontend calls incrementally
description: Replace centralized ApiFetch wrapper in src/App.tsx with an apiClient abstraction that can call either REST or JSON-RPC during migration. Add migration observability: every apiClient call logs transport=rest|rpc, method/feature, duration, success/failure, and error code if present. Migration order: (1) health/startup, (2) settings read/write, (3) leads list/detail/update, (4) scan start/stop/progress — proves task correlation and notification fan-out early, (5) profile, (6) ingestion, (7) generation/application, (8) remaining background events. Replace src/hooks/useWS.ts with a Tauri backend-event subscription hook only after streaming features are migrated. Exit criteria: no frontend code depends on port, API token, HTTP URL, or WebSocket; useWS is replaced; dual-transport logs make it clear which channel served each feature during migration.
